Unnamed: 19: Notes on Benefit
Unnamed: 20: Notes on Other Criteria

Click column heading for variable defini: Exemption for Senior Citizens with Local Option
Unnamed: 19: Localities may approve an additional exemption higher than the $150,000 in statute and set eligibility criteria for the additional exemption.
Up to 160 acres of residential property eligible.

Click column heading for variable defini: Home Exemption (Low Income Seniors, Local taxes)
Unnamed: 19: Up to 160 acres of residential property eligible. Benefit is full exemption if combined taxable income on federal tax return is not more than $12,000, but if over limit and AGI on state tax return is less than $12,000 max benefit is $5,000 assessed value exemption ($50,000 market value).

Click column heading for variable defini: Home Exemption (High Income Seniors, Local taxes)
Unnamed: 19: Exemption of up to $2,000 assessed value ($20,000 market value) applies to county taxes only. Local governments may opt to exempt $4,000 from assessed value ($40,000 market value). Up to 160 acres of residential property eligible.

Click column heading for variable defini: Elderly Assistance Fund: School Tax Reduction
Unnamed: 19: Benefit extends to homestead and up to 10 acres of land. Amount of benefit is based on the balance in the Elderly Assistance Fund; each year it is disbursed proportionately among all eligible property owners. Applicants must be enrolled in the Senior Valuation Protection Program (AZ 107). Income limit is $35,000 for a single individual.
Unnamed: 20: This program is only available in Maricopa County. Claimant must have occupied property as primary residence for two years. Applicants must live in an organized school district and be eligible for the property assessment freeze (AZ 107).

Unnamed: 19: The 50% exemption is only applied to the first $200,000 of actual value.
Unnamed: 20: Applicant must have owned homestead for the past ten years.

Click column heading for variable defini: Senior Citizen Property Owner Tax Relief
Unnamed: 19: If a properly completed and approved application is filed from April 1 to September 30, the property will receive one-half (50%) of the deduction reflected on the second installment (and full deductions for all tax years in the future).

Unnamed: 20: Income ceiling excludes up to $66,912 in income from retirement sources, pensions, or disability payments.

Click column heading for variable defini: School Property Tax Exemption for Elderly
Unnamed: 20: Income ceiling excludes up to $66,912 in income from retirement sources, pensions, or disability payments.

Click column heading for variable defini: State Homestead Exemption for the Elderly
Unnamed: 20: Eligibility is limited to the primary residence and up to 10 acres of land surrounding the residence.

Unnamed: 19: Maximum exemption is $5,000 of assessed value ($15,000 market value) except for Cook County where maximum exemption is $8,000 of assessed value ($24,000 market value).
The exemption is the lesser of  lesser of 50% of the assessed value of the real property, or $12,480. If co-owners are under 65 benefit reduced by a formula.
Unnamed: 20: Property's assessed value cannot exceed $182,430. Must have owned homestead for at least one year.

Unnamed: 19: Credit equals the amount that the current year's tax liability exceeds a 2% increase from prior year's tax.
Unnamed: 20: Homestead's assessed value cannot exceed $160,000. Income ceiling is $30,000 for individuals not married. Application deadline is March 31 for mobile home owners. Income for eligibility is established as adjusted gross income (AGI).

Click column heading for variable defini: Property Tax Relief for Low Income Seniors

Unnamed: 19: Base benefit is the greater of $2,000 exemption of taxable value or $175. Localities may increase benefit annually by CPI.
Unnamed: 20: Assets including real estate cannot exceed $20,000, although towns can increase asset limit to $40,000, exclude some home value, and adjust for inflation. Application deadline is later of April 1 or 3 months after tax bill is mailed.

Click column heading for variable defini: Exemption for Persons Aged 70 or Older (Clause 41)
Unnamed: 19: Benefit is greater of $500 credit or $4,000 exemption.
Unnamed: 20: Net worth excluding value of primary home cannot exceed $17,000 if single or $20,000 if married. Must have lived in the state for at least 10 years and owned property in the state for at least 5 years. Income ceiling is $6,000 for individuals not married.  Application deadline is later of April 1 or 3 months after tax bill is mailed.

Unnamed: 19: Recipients of this benefit are also exempted from the forest acreage tax.

Beneficiaries can take a $29,500 exemption from assessed value instead of the 50% exemption if it would result in greater tax savings (Applies if value < $59,000). Up to one acre of land is eligible for exemption.
Unnamed: 20: Claimants continue to receive benefit if residence unoccupied for medical reasons, including nursing home care.

Applicant must have resided in the state for at least three years. Local government sets asset limit, which must be at least $35,000 and exclude home value. Income ceiling is $13,400 for individuals not married.

Click column heading for variable defini: Annual Property Tax Deduction for Senior Citizens
Unnamed: 19: After initial application year, required annual form is due by Mar 1.
Credit equals the difference between property tax paid in the base year (i.e. first year eligible) and the amount paid in the current year.
Unnamed: 20: For 2018 benefit, 2017 income is subject to income limit of $70,000 and 2016 income may not exceed $87,000. Applicant must have owned homestead for past three years and lived in the state since 2006.

Unnamed: 19: The exemption amount on which the benefit is based is adjusted based on local housing prices, and is lower in the five largest cities (where it is applied to both general city taxes and school taxes). Savings from STAR cannot increase more than 2% per year. Residents of NYC are not eligible. The STAR exemption is now disbursed to new applicants as a rebate check (Starting in 2016), but homeowners who applied in prior years continue to receive STAR as a property tax exemption.

Unnamed: 20: The income ceiling only applies to individuals who turned 65 after 2013. There is no income ceiling for taxpayers who received this exemption in 2013.

Click column heading for variable defini: Property Tax Relief for the Elderly
Unnamed: 19: Claim must be submitted at least 35 days before taxes become delinquent.
This exemption is applied after the senior tax freeze (TX102).
The additional tax relief is granted to qualifying taxpayers who have applied for the Homeowner's credit circuit breaker.

Click column heading for variable defini: Senior Citizens' Homestead Property Tax Exemption
Unnamed: 20: Applicant must have resided in state for at least two years.

Income ceiling depends on household size: 1 person ($18,090), 2 ($24,360), 3 ($30,630), 4 ($36,900), 5 ($43,170), 6 ($49,440), 7 ($55,710), 8 ($61,800). Income ceilings are equal to 150% of federal poverty guidelines for 2017 (Source: HHS).

Click column heading for variable defini: Local Option Tax Relief for Seniors
Unnamed: 19: Relief under this program cannot exceed 10% of property value. If the total amount of tax relief provided by this program and other tax relief programs exceeds 75% of the taxes that would be owed without relief, then towns are required to put a lien on the property for the amount of tax relief exceeding 75%.
Unnamed: 20: Municipalities can set their own eligibility criteria and income requirements.

Unnamed: 19: Counties determine the amount of the credit, with a max benefit of $5,000. Kent County Levy Court provides up to a 100% exemption to applicants 65 or older or 100% disabled with income up to $22,000 if married ($16,000 if unmarried).
Unnamed: 20: Applicant must have lived in the state for at least three years. Income ceiling is $3,000 if unmarried.
Statute does not prescribe type of exemption (percentage or dollar amount)

Unnamed: 19: The max credit is $500. State will mail rebate checks directly to individuals who qualify for the program.
Unnamed: 20: Applicant must have lived in the state 10 years.

Click column heading for variable defini: Local Option Exemption for Seniors ($50k Exemption)
Unnamed: 19: Localities may offer instead of or in addition to FL107(b). The exemption applies only to taxes levied by the unit of government granting the exemption.

Click column heading for variable defini: Local Opt. Exemp. for Seniors w/Long-Time Residency
Unnamed: 19: Localities may offer instead of or in addition to FL107(a). The exemption applies only to taxes levied by the unit of government granting the exemption.
Unnamed: 20: To be eligible for a 100% exemption, the homeowner must have lived in the homestead for at least 25 years, and the value of the property must be less than $250,000 in the first year that the tax owner applies and is eligible for exemption.

Unnamed: 19: In Hawaii County, exemption is $80,000 for claimants age 60-69 and $100,000 for age 70+. In Kauai County, exemption is $180,000 for ages 60-70 and $200,000 for 71 and older. In the City and County of Honolulu, exemption is $120,000 for age 65+. Maui County does not offer an exemption based on age.
Unnamed: 20: Age criteria set locally.

Click column heading for variable defini: Local Option Senior Citizen Property Tax Refund
Unnamed: 20: No co-owner may be under 60 years of age.

Click column heading for variable defini: Tax Credit for Elderly with Limited Incomes
Unnamed: 20: Applicants must be 65 and have limited income but other criteria set locally.

Click column heading for variable defini: Municipal Property Tax Assistance (Senior Work Off)
Unnamed: 19: Maximum benefit is $750.
Unnamed: 20: Program allows localities to offer tax abatements to senior citizens who provide volunteer services. Additional criteria set locally.

Click column heading for variable defini: Local Option Senior Citizen Homestead Exemption
Unnamed: 19: Some localities may set a different application deadline.
Unnamed: 20: Applicant must have owned property for at least one year. Localities may set income ceiling between $3,000 and $29,000. Localities using a $29,000 income ceiling may offer exemptions of less than 50% to applicants with incomes over $29,000 but under $37,400 using the "sliding scale option." Income limit is $50,000 for NYC.

Click column heading for variable defini: Local Option Low-Income Deferral or Abatement
Unnamed: 19: Counties have option to grant either a $984 credit (maximum benefit for lowest income threshold for state circuit breaker program) or a 50% credit to low-income seniors, whichever is lower.
Unnamed: 20: To be eligible, claimants must have lived in the state 10 months. Benefit may be extended to applicants who are disabled and/or who demonstrate extreme hardship.
No more than 10 acres of land eligible.

Age: Local Option: Local governments set criteria.

Local Options: Inc: Can increase benefits or expand eligibility
Dec: Can decrease benefits or reduce eligibility
Both: Can increase or decrease benefits and eligibility
